slides.eyebrow: Invest Melbourne · Advisory Board · City of Melbourne 2026
slides.headline: Advisory Board Candidacy
slides.subheadline: Dr. Stephen Rando — an interview-preparation brief covering board alignment, credential mapping, anticipated questions and 2030 strategic contributions for Melbourne's $115 billion investment economy.
slides.cta: Board Brief · Credentials · Interview Q&A · 2030 Strategy

slides.headline: What this Board exists to grow
slides.subheadline: Anchor every answer to the scale, the horizon and the reach of the mandate.

slides.headline: Board purpose & your fit
slides.subheadline: The Invest Melbourne Advisory Board exists to attract and shape investment at city scale. Every response, credential and strategic idea you offer should map back to one of its five stated purposes.

slides.eyebrow: Map every answer back to these
slides.headline: The Board's stated purposes
slides.subheadline: Four are detailed in the brief; confirm the fifth purpose before the interview.

cards.title: A · Strategic advice
cards.body: Provide strategic advice and independent thinking to inform policy and productive investment in the municipality.

cards.title: B · Active advocacy
cards.body: Champion and proactively advocate for Invest Melbourne to Australian and international business communities, government departments and industry associations.

cards.title: C · Market insight
cards.body: Provide critical understanding of business, market and industry trends, with insights that improve strategic business development.

cards.title: D · Easier to operate
cards.body: Advise on ways to streamline business processes and make it easier for the business community to operate in and with the city.

slides.headline: I sit at the intersection of technology, governance, investment strategy and civic leadership — precisely where a Board of this kind needs to operate to make Melbourne's $115 billion economy both competitive and durable through 2030 and beyond.
slides.subheadline: Use as your anchor. Adapt the register, hold the substance.

slides.headline: What makes you distinctive
slides.subheadline: Three things this Board does not currently have. Frame these if asked what you would add — be direct; the panel will respect economy of language.

slides.eyebrow: Three things this Board does not have
slides.headline: Your distinctive contribution
slides.subheadline: Research frontier · Live relationships · Operator proximity.

cards.title: Applied AI governance, at the research frontier
cards.body: Active, peer-reviewed doctoral research producing frameworks that will inform national standards — not commentary on AI. A capability the Board can draw on so Melbourne's investment strategy is not behind the defining technology of the decade.

cards.title: Live Indo-Pacific connectivity — active, not archived
cards.body: Ongoing partnerships with IIT Palakkad, Deakin University and the Australia Africa Chamber of Commerce. Real relationships to real institutions, ready to serve Melbourne's international advocacy from day one.

cards.title: Proximity to the operators the Board serves
cards.body: Through GSR & Associates and direct fieldwork with manufacturing SMEs, current relationships with the $5–50M founder and operator cohort. I translate Board strategy into language they act on — and bring their voice back into deliberations.

slides.headline: Melbourne's $115 billion investment economy will be defined by those who understood that governance is not the cost of investment — it is the condition of it.
slides.cta: Dr. Stephen Rando · Invest Melbourne Advisory Board · City of Melbourne
slides.note: Deliver slowly. Let the final clause land before you stop.
